generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Captures real-time occupancy metrics within building spaces for dynamic DT updates. Integrated into BIM models by Building Control Engineers."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures environmental temperature across building zones for operational monitoring and energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance VibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Detects structural vibrations to monitor building integrity and equipment health in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VibrationDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks energy usage patterns for HVAC systems and other building subsystems to enable predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance EquipmentStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors operational status of building control systems (e.g., HVAC, lighting) for fault detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance VibrationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VibrationSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EquipmentStatusSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric and spatial representation of building assets derived from BIM platforms, serving as foundation for DT virtual representation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler, SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance BimSemanticModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantic layer enriched with device metadata and operational parameters from BIM, enabling structured data ingestion for DT operations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler, DataManagementEnabler
]

instance RealTimeSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of operational metrics (temperature, vibration, occupancy) collected via IoT sensors and SCADA systems."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, TemperatureDataTransmitted, VibrationDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataManagementEnabler, SimulationEngineEnabler, IntegrationFrameworkEnabler
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Archived performance data for buildings and subsystems used in predictive analytics and trend analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataManagementEnabler, AiMlEnabler
]

instance DeviceConfigurationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured representations of BCS components (sensors, actuators, controllers) generated via Model-Driven Engineering principles."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo IntegrationFrameworkEnabler, ControlAutomationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Building Information Modeling (CAD/BIM) platforms providing geometric, spatial, and semantic asset representation as DT foundation."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, VisualizationService, SimulationService
]

instance IoTSensorNetworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Internet of Things sensors and SCADA systems for real-time data collection from physical building infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, ControlAutomationService
]

instance DataManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Distributed databases (time-series) and cloud/edge hybrid architectures ensuring low-latency access to heterogeneous data sources."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation tools (finite element analysis) and AI/ML-driven predictive models for virtual testing and performance prediction."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService, OptimizationService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance IntegrationFrameworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware platforms (OPC UA, MQTT) enabling interoperability between legacy systems, IoT devices, and DT components."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlAutomationService, VisualizationService
]

instance AiMlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Supervised/unsupervised learning algorithms and digital twin-specific frameworks for predictive maintenance and adaptive control."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService, ControlAutomationService
]

instance VisualizationPlatformEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D/4D BIM viewers, AR/VR tools, and custom dashboards providing immersive interfaces for stakeholder decision support."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, SimulationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregates and processes runtime data (sensor readings, operational logs) to provide up-to-date insights into system performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, EquipmentAlertInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Uses historical and real-time data to forecast system failures, degradation, or optimization opportunities."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Applies algorithmic optimization to improve operational efficiency (energy use, space utilization) and reduce lifecycle costs."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyRecommendationAction, SpaceUtilizationInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables virtual testing of design modifications, refurbishment strategies, or adaptive changes through physics-based simulations."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignImpactInsight, RefurbishmentRecommendationAction
]

instance ControlAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrates with automation systems to adjust operational parameters based on DT-generated insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomatedControlAction, ClosedLoopOptimizationInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides interactive 3D/4D visualizations linking BIM models with real-time DT data for stakeholder communication."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveMonitoringInsight, CollaborationVisualizationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time overview of building operational metrics (energy consumption, structural health) for facility managers."
]

instance EquipmentAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Proactive alerts for equipment malfunctions or performance deviations detected through sensor data analysis."
]

instance FailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted equipment failures or system degradations based on predictive analytics models."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated suggestions for maintenance tasks derived from predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimized energy consumption strategies generated through algorithmic analysis of building performance data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SpaceUtilizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of occupancy patterns and space utilization efficiency across building zones."
]

instance DesignImpactInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Virtual simulation results showing potential impacts of design modifications on building performance."
]

instance RefurbishmentRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested refurbishment strategies based on simulation analysis of building systems."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AutomatedControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of building control systems (HVAC, lighting) based on real-time DT insights."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ClosedLoopOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous performance optimization metrics from automated control system adjustments."
]

instance ImmersiveMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D visualization of building operations with real-time data overlay."
]

instance CollaborationVisualizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation of shareable visual reports for stakeholder collaboration across building lifecycle stages."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system involves **control flows** originating from the digital twin to execute commands on physical building systems. Command types include **real-time monitoring directives** (e.g., adjusting HVAC setpoints, activating lighting systems, or triggering maintenance alerts) and **operational adjustments** (e.g., reconfiguring device parameters or optimizing energy usage). Control message formats are not explicitly detailed but are implied to adhere to **standardized protocols** supporting interoperability (as per NF_X). Validation and authentication mechanisms are inferred from the framework’s **non-functional requirements**, which prioritize **scalability and maintainability**, suggesting **role-based access control (RBAC)** and **message integrity checks** (e.g., digital signatures or checksums) to prevent unauthorized or erroneous commands. Feedback systems are enabled through **bidirectional data exchange**, where physical devices return status updates (e.g., execution confirmation, error codes, or performance metrics) to the DT for validation. Conflict resolution is handled via the **runtime configuration management** feature, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment, while command execution confirmation is ensured through **closed-loop validation** between the DT and physical systems. **Facility Managers** interact with the deployed DT to issue and monitor these commands."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling **low-latency operational monitoring and control**. The framework supports **dynamic temporal granularity** tailored to service-specific requirements, with **runtime configuration management** allowing adjustments to synchronization frequencies without full redeployment. While exact latency thresholds are not specified, the system is designed to bridge **static BIM representations** with **dynamic operational needs**, implying sub-second to near-real-time synchronization for critical building control and facility management services. The architecture prioritizes **interactive visualization** for immersive, time-sensitive monitoring, suggesting a focus on **high-frequency updates** for real-time decision-making in the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, role-based architecture** where the **DT Framework** serves as the **central orchestrator**, managing **bidirectional data flow** between enriched BIM models and operational Digital Twins. The **constellation** is structured around **four key roles**: **Building Control Engineers** (responsible for device modeling and initial deployment), **Facility Managers** (interacting with deployed Digital Twins for real-time operations), **DT Engineers** (extending capabilities via service integration), and the **DT Framework itself** (facilitating interoperability, scalability, and runtime adaptability). The system leverages **modular components** with **standardized extension points** to enable **dynamic service integration**, while **runtime configuration management** allows **on-the-fly adjustments** without full redeployment. The architecture adheres to a **requirements-driven, life-cycle-aware** approach, ensuring **holistic building management** from design to operations, with **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** addressing **interoperability, scalability, maintainability, and accessibility** across heterogeneous systems."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with semantic representations of Building Control Systems (BCS) components (e.g., **F1** in Table III).
  2. **Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The framework supports **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital twins, ensuring continuous synchronization.
  4. **Operation & Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to **monitor system states** and operate physical infrastructure via immersive interfaces (e.g., **interactive visualization**).
  5. **Customization & Evolution**: DT Engineers extend functionality through **custom services and plugins**, integrated via a **modular architecture** with standardized extension points (e.g., **F12** in Table III).
  Transition management is governed by **use-case-driven workflows** (Table II), ensuring alignment with stakeholder roles (Table I). Governance is implicit in the framework’s adherence to **BIM-DT interoperability** and **life-cycle-aware design** principles derived from research projects like **TwinLight and BOREALIS** (Section II)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The framework addresses **model fidelity** by enabling **Building Control Engineers** to define and configure BCS components (e.g., sensors, actuators, controllers) directly within BIM, ensuring semantic consistency between physical and digital representations. **Validation and verification** are implicit through the **automated generation of deployable DT artifacts** from enriched BIM models, reducing manual intervention and potential errors. **Uncertainty sources** may arise from BIM’s static limitations, which the framework mitigates via **real-time data exchange** to maintain synchronization. **Error detection and correction mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the modular architecture allows for **custom services and analytics** to be integrated for runtime quality assurance. **Quality assurance processes** are supported through the framework’s extension points, enabling stakeholders to validate DT functionality throughout its life cycle."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this DT system is designed to support **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** (as per NF_X) and is implied to feature a **modular, service-oriented architecture**. Communication protocols are not explicitly named but must align with **standardized extension points** for custom service integration, suggesting support for **industry-standard protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or REST APIs) for device communication. The network topology is likely **hybrid**, combining **edge computing** (for low-latency local processing) and **cloud/on-premise hosting** (for centralized analytics and storage). Bandwidth and latency requirements are inferred from the **real-time data exchange** mandate, necessitating **high-throughput, low-latency connections** (e.g., <100ms for critical commands). Reliability and redundancy mechanisms are implied by the **scalability and maintainability** NF_X requirements, including **failover protocols, load balancing, and distributed data replication**. Security protocols for network communication are not detailed but are critical for protecting data integrity and confidentiality, likely incorporating **encryption (TLS/SSL), authentication (OAuth, JWT), and network segmentation** to mitigate cyber threats."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this DT system supports **multi-platform hosting**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise** models, to accommodate varying operational needs. Computational requirements are driven by **real-time analytics, immersive visualization, and runtime configuration management**, necessitating **scalable compute resources** (e.g., containerized microservices or serverless functions) and **high-performance storage** for BIM models and operational telemetry. Scalability mechanisms are enabled by the **modular architecture**, allowing dynamic expansion of components (e.g., adding new sensors or analytics services) without full redeployment. Deployment models include **hybrid cloud-edge deployments**, where edge nodes handle localized processing (e.g., for latency-sensitive controls), while cloud platforms manage global analytics and user interfaces. Availability and reliability are assured through **redundancy, automated failover, and continuous monitoring**, aligning with the **maintainability and accessibility** NF_X requirements. The deployment process is initiated by **Building Control Engineers** using BIM tools, followed by **Facility Managers** and **DT Engineers** extending the framework via service integration."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **interoperability** with **external systems** through **standardized data exchange protocols** and **heterogeneous system integration**, enabling seamless connectivity with **BIM tools** (e.g., for device modeling by Building Control Engineers) and **enterprise systems** (e.g., facility management platforms). The system adheres to **interoperability standards** (implicitly referenced via NF_X requirements) to ensure compatibility with **third-party services and analytics**, with **modular extension points** allowing custom integrations. Dependencies on external systems are managed via the **DT Framework’s central hub**, which facilitates **bidirectional data synchronization** between physical assets and digital representations. The architecture supports **integration patterns** such as **service-oriented extensions** (e.g., DT Engineers adding custom analytics) and **runtime-adaptive configurations**, ensuring **low-overhead updates** and **scalability** across **individual components to entire infrastructure systems**. While specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, REST, MQTT) are not mentioned, the emphasis on **interoperability** implies adherence to **industry-standard APIs** for AECO domain systems."
]
